test_files.description: Unit tests for the new palette module: geo classification and palette family definitions

tests.name: classifyGeo returns 'tropical' for known tropical countries
tests.description: Verify that countries in the tropical belt (e.g. BR, TH, ID, NG) are classified as 'tropical'.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Import classifyGeo from src/visual/palette
Call classifyGeo('BR', 'SP')
Assert result equals 'tropical'
Repeat for 'TH', 'ID', 'NG'

tests.name: classifyGeo returns 'northern' for northern hemisphere countries
tests.description: Verify that northern/boreal countries (e.g. NO, SE, FI, IS) are classified as 'northern'.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Import classifyGeo from src/visual/palette
Call classifyGeo('NO', null)
Assert result equals 'northern'
Repeat for 'SE', 'FI'

tests.name: classifyGeo returns valid class for southern hemisphere countries
tests.description: Verify that southern hemisphere countries (e.g. AU, NZ, AR) are classified into 'southern' or 'oceanic'.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Import classifyGeo from src/visual/palette
Call classifyGeo('AU', null)
Assert result is one of the valid geo class strings

tests.name: classifyGeo returns 'unknown' for null country
tests.description: When country is null, classification should fall back to 'unknown'.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Fallback palette is used when geo hints are unavailable
steps: Call classifyGeo(null, null)
Assert result equals 'unknown'

tests.name: classifyGeo returns 'unknown' for unrecognized country codes
tests.description: Country codes not in the lookup table should fall back to 'unknown'.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Fallback palette is used when geo hints are unavailable
steps: Call classifyGeo('XX', null)
Assert result equals 'unknown'
Call classifyGeo('ZZ', 'AB')
Assert result equals 'unknown'

tests.name: every geo class has a valid palette family entry
tests.description: All possible return values from classifyGeo must have a corresponding palette family definition.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Import all geo classes and palette family lookup from src/visual/palette
For each geo class string, look up the palette family
Assert every class resolves to a non-undefined PaletteFamily object

tests.name: all palette family hueRanges are positive and at most 60
tests.description: Palette families should have constrained hue ranges to keep palettes cohesive.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Import all palette families
For each family, assert hueRange > 0 and hueRange <= 60

tests.name: all palette family saturationBase values are in 0.3-0.8
tests.description: Saturation base values should stay within the artistically defined range.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Import all palette families
For each family, assert saturationBase >= 0.3 and saturationBase <= 0.8

tests.name: all palette family hueCenters are in 0-360 range
tests.description: Hue centers must be valid hue angles.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Import all palette families
For each family, assert hueCenter >= 0 and hueCenter <= 360

tests.name: each distinct geo class maps to a palette family with a different hueCenter
tests.description: Different geo classes should produce visually distinct palette families.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Import all geo classes and their palette family mappings
Collect all hueCenters (excluding 'unknown' which may use full range)
Assert at least 4 distinct hueCenter values exist

test_files.description: Add US-010 specific test block to existing mappings test file and update existing tests for palette-family behaviour

tests.name: known tropical country produces hue in warm range
tests.description: A country classified as 'tropical' should produce a palette hue constrained to the warm family range (roughly 15-65°).
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with geo: { country: 'BR', region: 'SP' }
Assert paletteHue falls within the tropical palette family's expected hue range

tests.name: known northern country produces hue in cool range
tests.description: A country classified as 'northern' should produce a palette hue constrained to the cool family range (roughly 140-220°).
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with geo: { country: 'NO', region: null }
Assert paletteHue falls within the northern palette family's expected hue range

tests.name: null geo produces a valid fallback palette with full hue range
tests.description: When geo is null/null, the fallback palette should still produce valid hue and saturation values without throwing.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Fallback palette is used when geo hints are unavailable
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with geo: { country: null, region: null }
Assert paletteHue is in 0-360
Assert paletteSaturation is in 0.3-0.8
Assert no error is thrown

tests.name: same country and seed produces identical hue and saturation (deterministic)
tests.description: Palette values must be deterministic within a session — same inputs always produce same outputs.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Palette changes are deterministic within the session
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals twice with identical inputs including geo and seed
Assert paletteHue values are exactly equal
Assert paletteSaturation values are exactly equal

tests.name: same country with different seed produces different exact hue within same family
tests.description: The session seed should shift the exact hue within the palette family, so different sessions for the same country aren't identical.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Palette changes are deterministic within the session
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with geo 'BR' and seed 'seed1'
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with geo 'BR' and seed 'seed2'
Assert paletteHue values differ
Assert both hues are within the tropical family range

tests.name: different geo classes produce hues in different family ranges
tests.description: Countries from at least 3 different geo classes should produce hues in distinct, non-overlapping or minimally overlapping ranges.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals for a tropical country (e.g. BR)
Call mapSignalsToVisuals for a northern country (e.g. NO)
Call mapSignalsToVisuals for an oceanic/continental country
Assert at least 3 hues fall in distinctly different ranges (>30° apart)

tests.name: output VisualParams contains no raw country codes or region strings
tests.description: Privacy check: JSON serialization of VisualParams must not contain any raw geo identifier strings.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Raw geo values are not displayed in the scene
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with geo: { country: 'BR', region: 'SP' }
Serialize result with JSON.stringify
Assert serialized string does not contain 'BR', 'SP', or any country code
Repeat for several different geo inputs

tests.name: paletteSaturation stays within 0.3-0.8 for all palette families
tests.description: Saturation output must be bounded regardless of geo input.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals for multiple geo inputs: tropical, northern, null, unknown
For each result, assert paletteSaturation >= 0.3 and <= 0.8

tests.name: update T-008-03: different countries from different classes produce hues in different ranges
tests.description: Update existing test to verify that different geo classes produce hues in different family ranges, not just different exact values.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: Use countries from different geo classes (e.g. US=continental, JP=temperate, BR=tropical)
Assert hues fall in different ranges, not just differ by value
Keep the existing hues.size >= 2 check as a minimum bar

tests.name: update T-008-05: hue stays within expected family range for known countries
tests.description: Update existing range check test to verify hue is within the palette family range, not just 0-360.
tests.acceptance_criteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
steps: For each known-class geo input, verify hue is within that class's palette family hue range
For null geo, verify hue is within 0-360 (full fallback range)
Keep existing saturation range check
